Title: Comparative transcriptomics and metabolomics reveal specialized metabolite drought stress responses in switchgrass (Panicum virgatum)

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: Alamo AP13 reference genome annotation; expression compared in Alamo AP13 and Cave-in-Rock
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: class II diterpene synthase (diTPS); cis-trans-clerodienyl pyrophosphate synthase
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Root-enriched/co-expressed with furanoditerpenoid pathway genes under drought in Alamo; no drought-elicited changes in leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Roots, especially drought-treated Alamo roots at 2 and 4 weeks; little/no drought induction in Cave-in-Rock; no detectable drought-elicited changes in leaves
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes GGPP to cis-trans-clerodienyl pyrophosphate, providing the diterpene precursor scaffold for clerodane/furanoditerpenoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key branch-forming step supported; rate-limiting not established
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: panicoloids (panicoloid A, B, C; likely also related furanoditerpenoids)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports drought stress responses/tolerance in switchgrass roots
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with drought-induced accumulation of root furanoditerpenoids/panicoloids, especially in drought-tolerant Alamo
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative RNA-seq co-expression analysis with drought treatment; prior biochemical enzyme characterization cited; metabolite profiling and NMR structural identification of downstream products
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 4-week water-withholding drought treatment; root vs leaf tissue; genotype comparison between Alamo and Cave-in-Rock
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for engineering drought-associated root diterpenoid production, potentially by stacking with downstream CYP71Z genes and precursor-supply genes in drought-tolerant germplasm
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PvCPS1 showed drought-inducible co-expression in Alamo roots at 2 and 4 weeks. 2. PvCPS1 is a characterized CLPP synthase linked to clerodane/furanoditerpenoid biosynthesis. 3. Its expression pattern parallels drought-induced accumulation of panicoloid-type root diterpenoids, supporting a role in drought adaptation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper infers involvement in panicoloid biosynthesis from co-expression and prior pathway knowledge; direct in-paper functional proof for panicoloid formation was not performed.

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: Alamo AP13 reference genome annotation; expression compared in Alamo AP13 and Cave-in-Rock
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-expressed with PvCPS1 in drought-treated Alamo roots; little/no drought induction in leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root-associated drought-inducible expression in Alamo at 2 and 4 weeks; no strong induction in Cave-in-Rock; no leaf drought response detected
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: clerodane-related diterpene intermediates derived from CLPP
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes oxidative tailoring reactions leading to furanoditerpenoid formation from diterpene scaffolds
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Likely contributes to drought stress tolerance/response
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports formation and drought-induced accumulation of oxygenated root furanoditerpenoids in Alamo
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative RNA-seq co-expression under drought; prior biochemical characterization cited showing CYP71Z25 forms furanoditerpenoids; metabolite profiling/NMR of drought-induced diterpenoids
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 4-week drought treatment; root tissue; genotype comparison
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for pathway engineering of stress-associated root diterpenoids together with PvCPS1 and related CYP71Z genes
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP71Z25 was among P450 genes co-expressed with PvCPS1 in drought-treated Alamo roots. 2. Previous biochemical work identified CYP71Z25 as forming furanoditerpenoids. 3. The drought-inducible expression pattern supports its involvement in panicoloid-related metabolism and drought response.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Specific reaction steps toward panicoloids A-C were inferred, not directly reconstituted in this paper.

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: Alamo AP13 reference genome annotation; expression compared in Alamo AP13 and Cave-in-Rock
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-expressed with PvCPS1 in drought-treated Alamo roots
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Drought-inducible in Alamo roots at 2 and 4 weeks; little/no induction in Cave-in-Rock; no significant leaf response
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Performs oxidative tailoring in switchgrass furanoditerpenoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with root accumulation of drought-induced oxygenated diterpenoids
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative RNA-seq; prior biochemical characterization cited; LC-MS/NMR metabolite evidence
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential enzyme for combinatorial engineering of drought-associated diterpenoid blends in roots
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP71Z26 co-expressed with PvCPS1 in drought-stressed Alamo roots. 2. Prior studies support CYP71Z26 function in furanoditerpenoid formation. 3. This pattern links CYP71Z26 to panicoloid-related drought metabolites.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct assignment to individual panicoloids was not experimentally shown here.

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: Alamo AP13 reference genome annotation; expression compared in Alamo AP13 and Cave-in-Rock
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-expressed with PvCPS1 in Alamo roots under drought
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root drought-inducible in Alamo at 2 and 4 weeks; not strongly induced in Cave-in-Rock; no drought-elicited leaf expression changes detected
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes oxidative conversion steps in the formation of switchgrass furanoditerpenoids
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Correlates with drought-induced root accumulation of oxygenated clerodane furanoditerpenoids
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative RNA-seq; prior biochemical characterization cited; untargeted metabolomics and NMR characterization of products
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 4-week drought treatment; roots vs leaves; genotype comparison
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for engineering or marker-assisted selection of root stress-protective diterpenoid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP71Z28 showed drought-related co-expression with PvCPS1 in Alamo roots. 2. Previous work supports its role in forming furanoditerpenoids. 3. Co-expression with panicoloid accumulation implicates CYP71Z28 in drought-associated diterpenoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: As with CYP71Z25/26, exact panicoloid-specific chemistry remains inferential in this study.

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: Alamo AP13 reference genome annotation; expression compared in Alamo AP13 and Cave-in-Rock
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: class II diterpene synthase; predicted syn-copalyl pyrophosphate synthase
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-expressed in roots with PvCPS10, PvKSL4 and PvKSL5; not significantly drought-inducible
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root-expressed in Alamo; no strong drought-inducible transcript changes; leaf drought response not detected
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Predicted to cyclize GGPP to syn-CPP, precursor of syn-pimarane diterpenoids
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: syn-pimarane diterpenoids; broader drought-induced diterpenoid diversity
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Potential contribution to drought-associated root diterpenoid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Suggests existence of additional drought-associated diterpenoid pathways, though significant drought induction was not detected
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq co-expression analysis; pathway inference from prior diterpene synthase studies
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Secondary engineering candidate to diversify root diterpenoid blends, but metabolic effect under drought remains less directly supported than PvCPS1/CYP71Z genes
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PvCPS9 is a predicted syn-CPP synthase expressed with PvCPS10, PvKSL4 and PvKSL5 in roots. 2. These genes did not show significant drought-inducible transcript changes. 3. Their co-expression suggests a broader diversity of drought-associated root diterpenoids beyond panicoloids.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as core due to explicit discussion of its contribution to drought-induced diterpenoid diversity, but evidence is weaker than for PvCPS1-CYP71Z genes.

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: Alamo AP13 reference genome annotation; expression compared in Alamo AP13 and Cave-in-Rock
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: class II diterpene synthase; predicted syn-copalyl pyrophosphate synthase
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Root co-expression with PvCPS9, PvKSL4 and PvKSL5; not significantly drought-inducible
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in roots; limited/no significant drought-inducible change; no drought-leaf response reported
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Predicted to form syn-CPP from GGPP for downstream syn-pimarane diterpenoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: syn-pimarane diterpenoids; additional drought-associated root diterpenoids
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Potential contribution to drought-responsive diterpenoid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Suggests broader drought-associated diterpenoid biosynthetic capacity in roots
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq co-expression analysis; inference from prior biochemical pathway studies
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible pathway diversification target, though direct metabolite linkage under drought remains incomplete
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PvCPS10 co-expressed with syn-pimarane pathway genes in roots. 2. Significant drought-inducible changes were not observed. 3. The gene supports the presence of additional root diterpenoid pathways potentially relevant under drought.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Evidence level lower than for the panicoloid-linked genes.

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: Alamo AP13 reference genome annotation; expression compared in Alamo AP13 and Cave-in-Rock
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: class I diterpene synthase; kaurene synthase-like (KSL) enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Root co-expression with PvCPS9/PvCPS10 and PvKSL5; no significant drought induction
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root-expressed; no strong drought-inducible change; no detectable leaf induction
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts syn-CPP to syn-pimaradiene diterpene scaffolds
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports possible production of additional root diterpenoids during drought, though transcript induction was not significant
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq co-expression analysis; prior biochemical characterization cited showing syn-pimaradiene formation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to broaden stress-associated diterpenoid scaffolds in switchgrass roots
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PvKSL4 is a characterized class I diTPS that forms syn-pimaradiene compounds. 2. It co-expressed with PvCPS9 and PvCPS10 in roots. 3. This supports broader root diterpenoid diversity potentially relevant to drought response.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study did not directly assign specific drought-induced metabolites to PvKSL4 products.

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: Alamo AP13 reference genome annotation; expression compared in Alamo AP13 and Cave-in-Rock
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: class I diterpene synthase; kaurene synthase-like (KSL) enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Root co-expression with PvCPS9/PvCPS10 and PvKSL4; no significant drought induction
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in roots; not significantly drought-inducible; no leaf drought response reported
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts syn-CPP into syn-pimarane diterpene hydrocarbon scaffolds
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Potential role in root drought-associated metabolite diversification
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Indicates broader diterpenoid network potentially contributing to drought response, but without strong inducible transcript changes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq co-expression analysis; prior biochemical characterization cited
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential component of multi-gene engineering for diverse root diterpenoids
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PvKSL5 is a characterized syn-pimaradiene-forming class I diTPS. 2. It co-expressed with PvCPS9/PvCPS10 in roots. 3. Its pattern supports additional drought-associated diterpenoid pathway capacity in switchgrass.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct metabolite-to-gene linkage under drought was not resolved in this paper.

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in roots in Alamo but not Cave-in-Rock
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in Alamo roots, absent/not expressed in Cave-in-Rock roots
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: terpenoid backbone biosynthesis (MEP-derived diterpenoid precursor supply)
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: GGPP precursor supply to diterpenoid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: IPP and DMAPP / prenyl diphosphate intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Synthesizes GGPP, the universal C20 precursor for diterpenoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: root diterpenoids including panicoloids and related compounds
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: May contribute to genotype differences in drought-associated diterpenoid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression in Alamo but not Cave-in-Rock roots may contribute to ecotype-specific terpenoid metabolic differences
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Genotype comparison under drought/control; root tissue
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible precursor-supply engineering target to support enhanced root diterpenoid production, especially with downstream pathway genes
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A predicted GGPPS gene, Pavir.6NG089000, was expressed in Alamo roots but not in Cave-in-Rock roots. 2. Upstream terpenoid backbone genes were otherwise largely similar between genotypes. 3. This gene may contribute to genotype-dependent diterpenoid metabolic capacity.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper highlights this gene as an exception among largely unchanged precursor pathway genes; drought inducibility itself was not emphasized.
